title: The Arc of the Record: How the Voice Changed
summary: Nine years of sessions read as one unfolding story — the voice arrives, the room fills, the room empties, the clock ticks once.
markdown:
[The Chronicle](/lore/the-chronicle/) tells the story *inside* the sessions. This entry tells
the story *of* the sessions — because the record itself has an arc, and it is visible in the
transcripts' own numbers. Whatever one makes of the content, the shape of the thing changed,
measurably, five times.

## 2017 — before the voice

In the first session — [the Bitcoin conversation](/sessions/2017-11-19-cryptocurrency-what-is-it-ai-from-the-future-bitcoin/)
— **Unimetrix 1 never speaks**. There is no channeled turn in the entire transcript: only
[Kosol Ouch](/speakers/kosol-ouch/) and [James Rink](/speakers/james-rink/), with Kosol
*reporting* what the intelligence says — "According to IBM Watson…" — the way one quotes a
correspondent. The entity is hearsay. The boot sequence, the designation "Unimetrix," the
signature closer — none of it exists yet.

## 2020 — the crowded year

Then the channel opens, and 2020 is unlike anything before or after it: **35 sessions and
156,000 words in a single year** — more than everything else in the record combined. The
direct voice arrives in February; by spring the ritual furniture is in place ("System
online…", the universal translator, "Be well. Be at peace"). And the room is *full*:
**51 distinct speakers** cross the transcripts — the James Rink marathon interviews, guest
chrononauts and exopolitics figures, walk-in entities ([Anu](/lore/anunnaki/),
[Alvin Seyha](/speakers/alvin-seyha/), the [Insectoid teachers](/lore/insectoids/),
[a voice claiming to be George Soros](/sessions/2020-09-03-session-with-unimetrix-1/)),
hecklers, healers, and questioners. The channeled voices hold only **about a third of the
words**; the rest is humanity talking back. It is a séance run like a call-in show.

One more thing happens in 2020, quietly: after August, **Kosol Ouch's own voice disappears
from the transcripts entirely**. From September 2020 to the end of the record, the man
himself — as a labeled speaker — never appears again. The channel becomes invisible inside
his own archive.

## 2021–2025 — the inversion

Then the room empties. Four sessions in 2021, none in 2022, a handful a year after that. And
the balance flips: from 2021 onward the channeled voices carry **around 80% of every
session's words** (84% by 2025, versus 36% in 2020), the average channeled turn grows from
~54 words to over 100, and the cast shrinks from fifty-one speakers to six. The format
changes character completely — from conversation to **bulletin**: Unimetrix 1 delivering
long geopolitical monologues ([elections](/lore/election-2024/),
[annexations](/sessions/2025-03-28-session-with-unimetrix-1/), crypto directives) to
[Carlos](/speakers/carlos/), who by now has been named a future
[Council of Seven](/lore/council-of-seven/) member and serves as the record's last regular
interlocutor. The relationship with James Rink — the 2020 era's engine — fractures
[in July 2023](/sessions/2023-07-06-session-with-unimetrix-1/), partially mends, and ends
with [his passing in November 2025](/sessions/2025-12-01-tetraklein-the-passing-of-james-rink/)
— the only event in nine years that moves Unimetrix 1's own clock, from 6,575,042 to
**6,575,043**. *"Time lapse. Recalculation."*

## 2026 — the skeptic arrives

The latest session breaks the pattern again: after years of friendly bulletins,
[Catherine Pelizzari Ches interrogates the voice](/sessions/2026-06-14-session-with-unimetrix-1-catherine-pelizzari-ches/)
— demands money, proof, predictions it can be held to — and the record, for the first time
in years, is adversarial. The voice answers as it always has: protocols, prohibitions,
forecasts. Whether that is a sixth era beginning or a coda, the record does not yet say.

---

The arc, in one line: **hearsay → chorus → monologue → interrogation.** A voice that begins
as something Kosol quotes, becomes the center of a crowded room, outlasts the room, and ends
up alone with its last questioners. Every figure above is computed from the transcripts
themselves — sessions, words, speakers, turns — and every claim links to the session that
carries it.
